name: Gravel ride to Sanabria lake from Puebla de Sanabria
name_original: Circular gravel al lago de Sanabria desde Puebla de Sanabria
description:
Between the Sierra de la Culebra and Galicia, in the most remote part of the province of Zamora, is the largest glacial lake in Spain, Lake Sanabria. The landscape, bathed by the Tera and Castro rivers, boasts lush pine and oak forests, moors and a fascinating rural environment of fields that stands out when passing through its small towns.

The route we propose is circular from Puebla de Sanabria, a spectacular medieval enclave that has a well-preserved historical complex, among which the castle of the counts of Benavente and its popular architecture stand out. The city is well connected by train and road.

This route runs mainly along forest tracks and wide roads, although some stretches of asphalt also await you. This way, you can use almost any bike, although a specific one is ideal. Remember to bring some provisions with you so you can enjoy a stop in the middle of nature. From mid-spring to mid-autumn, each season offers its charm to ride through the Zamorano landscapes.
region_intro: Gravel biking in Zamora, Spain, offers a diverse landscape for off-road exploration. The region is characterized by a mix of rural plains, agricultural areas, and river valleys, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Riders can expect routes that traverse open countryside, often alongside historical towns and natural features. The elevation changes are generally moderate, with some routes featuring more significant climbs, making it suitable for different skill levels. Best no traffic gravel bike trails in Zamora The most popular no traffic gravel bike trail is Gravel ride to Sanabria lake from Puebla de Sanabria, a 23.9 miles (38.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging ride towards the scenic Sanabria lake. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Silos of Villalpando – Plaza Mayor of Villalpando loop from Villalpando, an easy 12.9 miles (20.7 km) path. This route explores the historical town of Villalpando and its surrounding agricultural landscapes. Local gravel bikers also love the Bodegas de Zorrilla – Morales de Rey loop from Morales de Rey, a 29.3 miles (47.1 km) trail leading through rural areas, often completed in about 3 hours 53 minutes. Gravel biking in Zamora is defined by rural landscapes, agricultural plains, and river valleys, with routes available for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Zamora's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic gravel bike trails are available in Zamora?
A: Komoot currently features 9 dedicated no-traffic gravel bike trails in Zamora. These routes are carefully selected to ensure a peaceful and car-free cycling experience, allowing you to fully immerse yourself in the region's natural beauty.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on Zamora's no-traffic gravel trails?
A: Zamora offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find everything from relatively flat, easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23745631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silos of Villalpando – Plaza Mayor of Villalpando loop&lt;/a&gt; with minimal elevation gain, to more challenging rides with significant climbs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1497896756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel ride to Sanabria lake from Puebla de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 600 meters of ascent. The region's landscape includes agricultural areas, river valleys, and mountainous sections, providing varied surfaces from smooth gravel to potentially looser, more technical stretches.

Q: Are there any easy, beginner-friendly no-traffic gravel routes in Zamora?
A: Yes, for those looking for a gentler introduction to gravel biking, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23745631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silos of Villalpando – Plaza Mayor of Villalpando loop from Villalpando&lt;/a&gt; is classified as easy. It covers just over 20 kilometers with minimal elevation, making it perfect for beginners or a relaxed ride through the countryside.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along these gravel trails?
A: Zamora's gravel trails often lead to stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You might encounter beautiful lakes, such as Sanabria Lake, or ride past significant geological formations. The region is also home to several impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/365687/waterfalls-in-zamora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abelón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored as highlights near some routes. Additionally, you may pass by unique man-made structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6063809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wind Turbines on Teso Santo&lt;/a&gt; or historical bridges.

Q: Are there any circular no-traffic gravel bike routes in Zamora?
A: Many of Zamora's no-traffic gravel routes are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to start and finish your ride in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23745631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silos of Villalpando – Plaza Mayor of Villalpando loop&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to explore the area without needing to retrace your steps.

Q: Can I access these gravel trails using public transport?
A: Public transport access varies depending on the specific trailhead. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1497896756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel ride to Sanabria lake from Puebla de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, starting points like Puebla de Sanabria are generally accessible by bus, though it's advisable to check local schedules and bike transport policies in advance. For routes closer to larger towns like Zamora city, local buses might offer connections to trailheads.

Q: What is the best time of year to go gravel biking in Zamora?
A: The best time for gravel biking in Zamora is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, with less extreme heat than summer and fewer chances of heavy rain or snow than winter. This allows for comfortable riding conditions and vibrant natural scenery.

Q: Are there any family-friendly no-traffic gravel bike trails?
A: Yes, for families, easier routes with less elevation and shorter distances are ideal. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23745631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silos of Villalpando – Plaza Mayor of Villalpando loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, offering a relatively flat and manageable ride through scenic agricultural landscapes, suitable for various skill levels within a family.

Q: Where can I find parking for these no-traffic gravel bike trails?
A: Parking availability depends on the specific starting point of each trail. Many routes begin in or near towns and villages, where public parking is often available. For example, routes starting from Puebla de Sanabria or Villalpando typically have designated parking areas. It's recommended to check the route details on Komoot for specific parking information near your chosen trailhead.

Q: What is the difficulty range of the no-traffic gravel bike trails in Zamora?
A: The no-traffic gravel trails in Zamora cater to a wide range of abilities. You'll find easy routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23745631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silos of Villalpando loop&lt;/a&gt;, which are suitable for beginners. There are also moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monoliths of the Three Causeways – Zamora Stone Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt;, and more difficult options, including the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1497896756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel ride to Sanabria lake&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant climbs and longer distances.
tips: Built in the s. XV by the Pimentel family after taking control of the region, the fortress would witness the wars with Portugal in the s. XVIII and the War of Independence, when Portuguese soldiers would face the Serrar Division of the French army.
It is a beautiful natural path through the water mills of the Tera River from Lake Sanabria to Galende.

Beautiful views of Lake Sanabria from Playa de los Arenales, also known as Playa de Los Enanos. If we are in the area, it is worth going to this point, as it has a more open space from where you can see the lake, sandy soil with stones, a large green area and surrounding trees where you can take refuge from the high temperatures. It is advisable to wear some type of water shoes since the first meters of the bathing area have stones, but it is not absolutely necessary. Parking is very ample, as the driveway is very wide. To have a drink, there is a beach bar at the exit of the beach and another in the parking area.
At this point there are wonderful views of the flow of the Tera river before it merges with the Sanabria lake. An extraordinary lake of glacial origin that is the largest in Spain.
We are near the lake of Sanabria rolling along the MTB route Puebla - El lago, where we will have some picturesque views through the beautiful and dense forest.

Located in the homonymous region, this picturesque town is the ideal location to stay during a visit to the surrounding towns and places. Declared an asset of cultural interest for its historical architectural complex, it has been a military bastion for decades, since the topography favors its defense, still preserving part of its wall.

The Castle of the Counts of Benavente, the Romanesque church of Sta. María del Azogue and the hermitage of S. Cayetano, are places of tourist interest.
